pattern: Sorting plus a two-pointer sweep for each fixed element finds all distinct zero-sum triplets while skipping duplicates in quadratic time.
hints: Fix one number and the problem becomes finding two others that sum to its negation.
After sorting, a left/right pointer pair can hunt for the needed complement, and equal neighbors can be skipped to avoid duplicate triplets.

Sort the array. For each i (skipping repeats of nums[i]), use l=i+1 and r=n-1: if the triple sum is negative advance l, if positive retreat r, and on zero record the triplet then skip duplicate l and r values.

edge_inputs.description: classic mix with one duplicate triplet to suppress
all zeros yields a single triplet, not many
all zeros with duplicates still one triplet
minimum length with a valid triplet
many duplicates produce few distinct triplets

stress.description: moderate array over a small value range so many triplets and duplicates appear
stress.note: narrow value range maximises duplicate-suppression stress
